search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae tinea pedis issue 2 features red flags investigation follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic topical or oral antifungal advice
automatic hygiene, reinfection, microscopy, culture, biopsy, or referral instruction
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: No medicine, dose, duration, treatment or autonomous advice was mapped.
All tests and referral decisions remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Primarily adults and adolescents with clinician-suspected or established tinea pedis; diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, pregnancy, children, and atypical disease need qualified review.
setting_applicability: UAE primary, telehealth, podiatry, and dermatology follow-up.
UAE_applicability: The primary source is UAE DHA guidance; local medicine, laboratory, diabetes, infection, podiatry, and referral pathways remain required.
superseded_check: No newer DHA tinea-pedis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact UAE DHA section supports documenting a tinea-pedis review, interval course, itch, scale, fissures, maceration, redness, vesicles, ulcers, foot distribution, clinician inspection and impact without inferring active fungal infection.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports documenting severe pain, diabetes, immunosuppression, spread, secondary infection and systemic illness only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe pain, hot red foot, diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, secondary infection, systemic illness, extensive disease, and non-response.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation section supports recording laboratory results already available without generating microscopy, culture or biopsy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes clinician-selected microscopy, fungal culture, bacterial culture, and rare biopsy when diagnosis is uncertain or atypical.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact sites and morphology, nail or body spread, diabetes and immune status, prior treatment and response, diagnosis, KOH or culture, medicine, dose, duration, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
